name: ROCm GPU Monitor
description: Read-only AMD GPU monitoring for Claude on Linux — VRAM, temperature, clocks, power, and running ML processes via rocm-smi.
long_description:
This extension lets Claude answer questions like "is my training run healthy?", "how hot is the GPU?", "how much VRAM is left?", and "which process is using the card?" on a Linux machine with an AMD GPU. It shells out to rocm-smi (and optionally amdgpu_top) and exposes a small set of read-only tools. No process killing, no clock/power overrides, no fan control — if you need those, use a tool like gpu-kill.

**Requirements:** An AMD GPU with the amdgpu kernel driver loaded and ROCm installed (`sudo apt install rocm-smi` or a full ROCm stack). Works with RDNA2/3/4 consumer cards (RX 6000/7000/9000, R9700) and CDNA data-center cards (MI series). Some metrics require newer ROCm versions; the server gracefully reports which metrics are unsupported on your card.

**Scope:** Strictly read-only. All tools carry the MCP `readOnlyHint: true` annotation. Safe to enable on machines running long training jobs — nothing this extension does can interrupt a run.

tools.name: gpu_status
tools.description: One-shot summary of all AMD GPUs on the system: product name, GPU utilization %, VRAM used/total, edge/junction temperatures, average power draw, fan speed.

tools.name: gpu_metrics
tools.description: Full rocm-smi metrics dump for every GPU (clocks, voltages, PCIe link, firmware versions, per-engine activity). Use for deep debugging when gpu_status is not enough.

tools.name: gpu_processes
tools.description: List GPU processes (KFD PIDs) with their VRAM usage. Returns an empty list if no compute workloads are running.

tools.name: gpu_watch
tools.description: Sample gpu_status at a fixed interval for a bounded duration and return the time series plus per-sample deltas. Useful for checking whether a training run is steady or fluctuating.

tools.name: rocm_info
tools.description: Report the rocm-smi version, kernel driver version, loaded amdgpu module, installed ROCm packages (dpkg), and whether amdgpu_top is available.
